start.line: Lamp's dead, and my lens with it. The crate went off the end of the pier this morning, hoist and all.

options.text: Your lens is in the harbour?
Can't you fish the crate out?
I got the lens up. It's on my back.
I'll see what I can do. (leave)

crate.line: Aye. Straight down off the pier end, just past my skiff. Too deep to swim, too heavy to want to.

options.text: Can't you fish it out yourself?

fish.line: These arms are old rope, lad. But there's good line about the dock, and my boathook's on the rail — a strong back could rig something.

raised.line: Ha! Then what are you standing here for? Up the stairs with it — the housing's open and waiting.
